committee_roles.label: Product Manager Committee
committee_roles.responsibility: Challenge user value, scope discipline, sequencing, and acceptance criteria before implementation starts.

committee_roles.label: Technical Architect Committee
committee_roles.responsibility: Challenge architecture, interfaces, migration cost, validation strategy, and operational safety.

committee_roles.label: User Committee
committee_roles.responsibility: Challenge the plan from concrete usage scenarios, day-to-day friction, trust, and clarity.

product_context.target_outcome: Turn `autonomous-dev-loop-kit` into a stronger cross-CLI kit that gathers high-quality project data and context before acting, then challenges each version through committee-driven review. Make high-quality data acquisition and committee-driven requirement review first-class parts of the loop, not implicit side effects. Keep the kit usable from minimal prompts such as `循环10`, while preserving strict validation, reporting, and Git publication rules. Strengthen the committee architecture with clearer council roles, structured scope decisions, durable dissent, and evaluator-style readiness checks without turning the kit into heavyweight enterprise process.
constraints: Keep the core protocol shared across Codex CLI and Claude Code CLI.
Use ReAct in the Shunyu Yao paper sense: reason, act, observe, update.
Do not require verbose chain-of-thought in user-visible chat.
Every published version must pass real validation, write a report, and publish to this repo's own GitHub remote.
Favor deterministic scripts and durable repo state over purely prompt-based behavior.

research_findings: The current config only models three flat committee roles, so it cannot distinguish council membership from secretariat or evaluator responsibilities.
The next architecture step should add a lightweight V2 roster in config first, while keeping the existing roles array so current rendering and validation do not break.
A persona-id based roster is enough for this round; detailed field contracts and richer rendering can wait for the next iteration.
committee_feedback: Product council wants clearer responsibility boundaries so product, architecture, operator, and evaluator roles stop blending together in later workflow steps.
Architecture council wants the stronger structure to stay backward-compatible, because render-committee.py and current validators still depend on the existing roles list.
Operator council wants the stronger model to remain lightweight and readable in config, not explode into a large prompt registry in one step.
committee_decision: Add a concise committee V2 config layer with councils, persona ids, secretariat, evaluator, rubric hook, and escalation policy while preserving the existing roles roster for current tooling.
latest_review_state.goal_title: Define lightweight councils, secretariat, and evaluator roster in config
latest_review_state.current_goal_title: Define lightweight councils, secretariat, and evaluator roster in config

notes: This snapshot is a first-pass repo scan and should be refreshed after major structural changes.
